title: Rubio to meet with Congress as Iran ceasefire falters
description: The secretary of state will testify publicly for the first time since the war began, as the regional conflict worsens and lawmaker patience runs thin.

title: Ranking the 2028 Republican presidential contenders
description: Vance and Rubio top the list but there’s plenty of room for an anti-Trump candidate.

title: Rep. Jim Himes, D-Conn., shares his thoughts on the U.S.-Iran peace talks
description: NPR's Leila Fadel asks Democratic Rep. Jim Himes of Connecticut, ranking member of the House Intelligence Committee, for his view of the U.S.-Iran ceasefire and negotiations.

title: Stripping U.S. citizenship en masse is harder than Trump vowed
description: President Trump's vow to revoke citizenship worries immigrant advocates, legal scholars and naturalized Americans — but so far it's proving harder to do than the rhetoric suggests.

title: For veterans, a place where peace can take root
description: Iraq war veteran John Follmer leads vet volunteers who are rehabbing a neglected Japanese garden on the West LA Veterans Affairs Campus.

title: Tuesday is a big primary day. Here are key races to watch
description: Six states — California, Iowa, Montana, New Jersey, South Dakota and New Mexico — hold elections on Tuesday. Here are key races to follow.

title: Political parties once avoided primaries. In many places, that's changed
description: For years, political parties in most states have avoided getting involved in primary races -- leaving the choice to voters. But in many places, those elections are looking different this year.

title: Tom Suozzi, D-N.Y., on the DOJ pausing its 'anti-weaponization' fund
description: NPR's Leila Fadel asks Democratic Rep. Tom Suozzi of New York about the DOJ's statement that it will respect a court ruling temporarily blocking President Trump's "anti-weaponization" fund.

title: Trump ally convicted of in Colorado vote tampering case released from prison early
description: A county clerk in Colorado convicted of interference in the 2020 elections is being released from jail early after President Trump pressured the state's Democratic governor.

title: Justice Department says it will abide by court order pausing its 'anti-weaponization' fund
description: The Justice Department said it 'strongly disagrees' with the court's ruling that paused a $1.776 fund for victims of government "weaponization," but would still abide by it.

title: Graham Platner says voters are 'forgiving'
description: Controversy seems to follow democratic senate candidate Graham Platner. He tells Morning Edition host Leila Fadel voters are forgiving and concerned about their own lives.

title: With competitive midterm elections, Iowa Democrats say they're not in disarray
description: Iowa Democrats will decide their nominee for a competitive U.S. Senate race Tuesday. The state also has surprisingly competitive elections for U.S. House and the governor's office in November.

title: The bizarre race to succeed California Gov. Gavin Newsom
description: Its unusual primary system, combined with scandal and a low-profile field, have made this year’s race harder to predict.

title: Who’s leading the wide-open 2028 Democratic presidential field?
description: The early field is still wide open.

title: Spanberger’s veto spree turns many in her own party against her
description: The Virginia governor is being attacked from all sides for actions she considers moderate, including 31 vetoes of bills from the Democratic-led legislature.
